meaning: 鲁阿佩胡火山[新西兰](在北岛中南部)
examples: The image shows Taupo's Mount Ruapehu erupting in June 1996.
Ruapehu itself was not shown in the flint, out of respect for the Maori people. who regard it as a sacred site.
The nature of volcanic activity in the area is different from the subduction-driven volcanism elsewhere on the North Island, such as at Mount Ruapehu.
Then came New Zealand with Tongariro National Park in 1887, protecting the Tongariro, Ngauruhoe and Ruapehu volcanoes.
